name: Account Tampering - Suspicious Failed Logon Reasons
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Account Tampering - Suspicious Failed Logon Reasons
SIGMA Description: This method uses uncommon error codes on failed logons to determine suspicious activity and tampering with accounts that have been disabled or somehow restricted.

False positives:
- User using a disabled account

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND (("Event ID" IN('4625', '4776')) AND (Status IN('0xC0000072', '0xC000006F', '0xC0000070', '0xC0000413', '0xC000018C', '0xC000015B'))) AND (NOT(LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%s-1-0-0%')) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Account Tampering - Suspicious Failed Logon Reasons on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND (("Event ID" IN('4625', '4776')) AND (Status IN('0xC0000072', '0xC000006F', '0xC0000070', '0xC0000413', '0xC000018C', '0xC000015B'))) AND (NOT(LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%s-1-0-0%')) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Account Tampering - Suspicious Failed Logon Reasons
